description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a distinctive, textured appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark coloration that contrasts with the lighter, speckled stone surface on top. The grooves appear to be cut with precision, suggesting a functional or decorative purpose, possibly for drainage or anti-slip in an urban environment. The block is robust and heavy, likely made from granite or a similar dense stone material. Its design is utilitarian, suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in public spaces such as sidewalks, plazas, or park boundaries.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, featuring a dark, solid base and a speckled stone finish. The grooved pattern suggests a functional or decorative urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the solid, dark base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or anti-slip surface
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous barrier or edge along a paved area
